2605.21758.title: A Causal Argumentation Method for Explainability of Machine Learning Models
2605.21758.paper_summary: Explainable AI (XAI) methods identify which features are relevant to a model's predictions but often fail to clarify why certain decisions are made. In this work, we present a novel method that integrates causality with argument-based reasoning to explain why models may be making predictions. Our approach first identifies causal relationships among variables using causal discovery methods and then translates these into a Bipolar Argumentation Framework (BAF) to represent supportive and opposing interactions among features. By using semi-stable semantics, we find extensions of features that explain why certain outcomes may have been chosen. We demonstrate our method on two benchmark datasets and compare its results against standard post-hoc explainability approaches.
2605.21758.paper_summary_zh: 可解釋的人工智慧（XAI）方法能夠識別哪些特徵與模型的預測相關，但通常無法澄清為什麼會做出某些決策。在本研究中，我們提出了一種新方法，將因果性與基於論證的推理相結合，以解釋模型為何可能會做出預測。我們的方法首先使用因果發現方法識別變數之間的因果關係，然後將這些關係轉換為雙極論證框架（BAF），以表示特徵之間的支持和反對互動。通過使用半穩定語義，我們找到了特徵的擴展，解釋為什麼某些結果可能被選擇。我們在兩個基準數據集上演示了我們的方法，並將其結果與標準的事後可解釋性方法進行比較。
2605.21758.author: Henry Salgado et.al.
2605.21758.authors: Henry Salgado, Meagan R. Kendall, Martine Ceberio
